name: Sensorama
short_description: Morton Heilig's 1962 multi-sensory arcade cabinet — stereoscopic film, spatial sound, vibration, wind, and scent — the earliest physical prototype of immersive, body-engaged virtual experience.
long_description: Designed and built by filmmaker Morton Heilig, the Sensorama was a coin-operated cabinet that immersed a single viewer in one of five short films using stereoscopic 3-D color imagery, directional stereo sound, a vibrating seat, fans that generated wind, and chemical odor emitters timed to the film content (exhaust fumes on a Brooklyn motorcycle ride, sea air on the beach). Heilig filed the patent in January 1961; it was granted as U.S. Patent 3,050,870 in 1962. No AI or computation was involved — the experience was fully pre-recorded and mechanically synchronized — yet the Sensorama established the design vocabulary of full-body, multi-modal immersive experience that VR, mixed reality, and spatial computing would inherit decades later. Financial backing was never secured and the project remained a one-off prototype.
ai_note: A fully pre-recorded, mechanically synchronized experience with no computation or AI of any kind. Its significance is as the physical-design origin point for immersive, multi-sensory virtual experience.

images.caption: Figure 5 from Morton Heilig's Sensorama patent (U.S. Patent 3,050,870, 1962), showing the cabinet design.
images.alt_text: A patent drawing showing the Sensorama cabinet in cross-section: a viewer seats themselves at the top and looks into an eyepiece hood while the internal projection and sensory apparatus occupies the lower body of the machine.

sources.title: Sensorama — Wikipedia
sources.access_notes: Overview of the device, its sensory channels (stereoscopic 3-D, stereo sound, vibration, wind, scent), the five short films, and the patent (U.S. #3,050,870).

sources.title: Morton Heilig — Wikipedia
sources.access_notes: Biographical details on Heilig (December 22, 1926 – May 14, 1997): filmographer, VR pioneer; developed Sensorama from 1957; patent filed January 10, 1961, granted 1962.

sources.title: U.S. Patent 3,050,870 — Sensorama Simulator
sources.access_notes: Primary patent document filed January 10, 1961; granted August 28, 1962. Describes the full multi-sensory cabinet design including olfactory, tactile, visual, and audio channels.
